THE MAGICAL TEIDE - THE SACRED VOLCANO THAT HOLDS UP THE DOME OF STARS
This iconic route through Teide National Park combines geology, mythology, and dramatic
landscapes.
ROUTE TYPE: Circular hiking route (volcanic landscape trail)
ESTIMATED DURATION: 2–2.5 hours. DISTANCE: Approx. 3.5–4 km
START/END POINT: La Ruleta Viewpoint
ROUTE OVERVIEW (STOPS)
1. La Ruleta Viewpoint (start)
2. Roque Cinchado
3. El Torrotito & El Burro rock formations
4. Descent toward Llano de Ucanca
5. Llano de Ucanca (volcanic plain)
6. La Catedral (Roques de García formation)
7. Ascent back to La Ruleta (sea of clouds viewpoints)
8. La Ruleta Viewpoint (end)
VISITABLE PLACES & OPENING HOURS:
(󽁔󽁕󽁖 Access conditions may change due to weather, conservation rules, or safety restrictions. Always
check official park before visiting.)
• Roques de García Trail (this route): open access; no fixed hours, but recommended during
daylight only for safety.
• La Ruleta Viewpoint & Llano de Ucanca: public viewpoints, accessible at all times.
PRACTICAL TIPS FOR THE ROUTE
• Footwear: Wear sturdy hiking shoes—terrain is uneven and volcanic.
• Altitude awareness: You’ll be above 2,000 m—walk at a comfortable pace and stay
hydrated.
• Sun protection: UV radiation is intense at this altitude—bring sunscreen, sunglasses, and a
hat.
• Weather: Conditions can change quickly—bring layers (wind and temperature drops are
common).
• Stay on marked trails: The ecosystem is fragile and protected.
• Best time to visit: Early morning or late afternoon for softer light and fewer crowds.
• Photography: This is one of Tenerife’s most photogenic areas—allow extra time for stops.
This route offers a unique journey through one of the most extraordinary volcanic landscapes in the
world, where science, legend, and raw natural beauty converge under one of the clearest skies on
Earth.
